author: draganrakita
content: You are already breaking user assumptions with eip8037 new state pricing. Overcharging is already broken.

author: draganrakita
content: And I don't follow the notion that builders will need to do more, or do something for free. Proposal is for builders to do exactly what is needed and all that work is covered by gas.

author: draganrakita
content: I am just applying what we do inside the frame to pre execution (eip7703 list and target loading). And unifying those two.

author: lu_pinto
content:
So I may be confused about your definition of intrinsic and runtime cost:
> Intrinsic gas for authorization list covers: signature recovery and tx bytes.
> - Runtime gas should cover warm/cold load and state creation.

So builders will only find out if a tx is invalid in certain cases if they actually try to process the authorization list, as opposed to validating for worst case knowing it is going to be valid for sure? Like from what you mentioned account writes are not part of the intrinsic cost part (which is used for tx validation on selection).
The extra work I'm talking about is the authorization list processing if user doesn't have the funds to do it.

author: draganrakita
content:
Idea is to included tx in any way, and user would pay intrinsic part of it. But for the case where there is runtime gas for first 2 auth items but not for last N items, we can decide what to do:
1. Revert all auth items.
2. Skip last N auth items. only first 2 auth items are applied.
And of course skip execution and consume all of the gas.
